environment: Vagrant 2.4.9, vagrant-parallels 2.4.7, Parallels Desktop 26.1.1, bento/ubuntu-24.04 arm64
notes: The VM accepted --nested-virt on, but prlctl start failed before guest boot with Unable to start the virtual machine. The Vagrantfile now defaults Parallels nested virtualization off and allows explicit opt-in through NANOFUSE_PARALLELS_NESTED=1.

environment: Vagrant 2.4.9, vagrant-parallels 2.4.7, bento/ubuntu-24.04 arm64, forwarded API port 8080=>18080
notes: Updated Vagrantfile validated and forwarded guest API port 8080 to host 18080. Guest boot and rsync succeeded. Provisioning failed at setup preflight with /dev/kvm not found, so guest-side mage ci, nanofused health, capabilities, and Firecracker verify.sh could not run on this host. VM was halted after the run.

command: vagrant ssh KVM check; swift Virtualization nested support check; prlctl set --nested-virt on/start/off/start
environment: Apple M2 Max, macOS 26.5 build 25F71, Parallels Desktop 26.1.1, Vagrant default Ubuntu 24.04 aarch64 VM
notes: Guest reported KVM_MISSING. Apple Virtualization returned false for nested virtualization support. Parallels accepted --nested-virt on, but prlctl start failed with Unable to start the virtual machine. The VM was restored with nested virtualization off and is running again.
